rooting_indicator_1: White root nubs visible at nodes within 7-10 days in water
rooting_indicator_2: New leaf growth emerging from cutting
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White healthy roots 2-5 cm long visible; new leaves emerging
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning brown/black/mushy at cut end; leaves yellowing and dropping
root_development_notes: Aerial roots at nodes are pre-formed and speed up rooting dramatically; always include a node with aerial root for fastest results

rooting_indicator_1: White root tips visible at node within 14-21 days in water
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White healthy roots 3-8 cm long; new leaf unfurling
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning black/mushy at cut end; node becoming soft and discolored
root_development_notes: Aerial roots at nodes contain pre-formed root initials making them root faster; cuttings without a node will never root regardless of time

rooting_indicator_1: Small white root nubs visible at cut end or node after 3-4 weeks
rooting_indicator_2: New bud or leaf emerging from cutting
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White or cream colored roots 2-5 cm long; new bud swelling
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning black/brown at cut end; leaves yellowing and dropping; mushy stem base
root_development_notes: Milky latex sap from cut end should be allowed to dry for 1-2 hours before planting to prevent rot; bottom heat (25-28°C) significantly improves rooting speed

rooting_indicator_1: White root nubs visible at node within 14-21 days
rooting_indicator_2: New bud or shoot emerging from cane node
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White healthy roots visible; new shoot or bud emerging from node
failed_rooting_signs: Cane turning soft and mushy; foul smell from rooting medium; no bud emergence after 6 weeks
root_development_notes: Even bare leafless cane sections will sprout roots and shoots from nodes if kept moist and warm; always wear gloves when handling as sap causes skin and oral irritation

rooting_indicator_1: White root nubs visible at stem base within 14-21 days
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White healthy roots 2-5 cm long; new leaf emerging
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning brown/black at cut end; leaves yellowing and wilting; mushy base
root_development_notes: Division is the most reliable rooting method as offsets already have established roots; stem cuttings root best with bottom heat and high humidity

rooting_indicator_1: White root nubs visible at nodes within 7-14 days in water
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White healthy roots 2-5 cm long visible; new leaf emerging
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning brown/black at cut end; leaves yellowing and dropping
root_development_notes: Aerial roots at nodes speed up rooting significantly; one of the easiest plants to root in water

rooting_indicator_1: White root tips visible at cut end or node after 3-5 weeks
rooting_indicator_2: New bud or leaf emerging from cutting
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White/cream fibrous roots 2-5 cm long; new bud swelling and leaf emerging
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning black/brown; leaves dropping completely; cutting shriveling
root_development_notes: Bottom heat (22-25°C) and high humidity dome significantly improve rooting; hardwood cuttings taken in autumn root more reliably than softwood summer cuttings

rooting_indicator_1: White root tips visible at cut end after 3-4 weeks
rooting_indicator_2: New bud or leaf emerging from cutting
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White healthy roots 2-5 cm long; new leaf bud swelling
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning black at base; leaves dropping; stem shriveling
root_development_notes: Semi-hardwood cuttings (firm but not fully woody) root more reliably than soft tip cuttings; keeping humidity high around cutting while avoiding waterlogged medium is key

rooting_indicator_1: White root tips visible at cut end after 3-4 weeks
rooting_indicator_2: New leaf bud swelling and emerging
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White healthy roots 2-5 cm long; new leaves emerging
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning black at base; leaves dropping; cutting shriveling and drying out
root_development_notes: Semi-hardwood cuttings with 2-3 nodes root best; keeping high humidity around cutting using a plastic bag or dome improves success significantly

rooting_indicator_1: White root tips visible at cut end after 4-6 weeks
rooting_indicator_2: New bud or leaf emerging from cutting
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White healthy roots 2-5 cm long; new bud emerging; leaves remaining firm and colorful
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning black/brown at base; leaves dropping; milky sap oozing from rotting stem
root_development_notes: Milky sap from cut end should be allowed to dry for 1-2 hours before planting; Croton is prone to transplant shock and leaf drop even when rooting is successful; humidity dome recommended

rooting_indicator_1: White root tips visible at cut end after 5-8 weeks
rooting_indicator_2: New bud or compound leaf emerging from cutting
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White fibrous roots 2-5 cm long; new compound leaf emerging
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning black/brown at base; no new growth after 10 weeks; cutting drying out or rotting
root_development_notes: One of the more difficult plants to root from cuttings; root suckers from base of established plants are much more reliable; fresh seeds germinate far more easily than cuttings root

rooting_indicator_1: White root nubs visible at nodes within 5-10 days in water
rooting_indicator_2: New leaf emerging vigorously from cutting
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the cutting
successful_rooting_signs: White healthy roots 1-3 cm long; new leaves emerging vigorously
failed_rooting_signs: Stem turning black at base; leaves wilting and dropping; foul smell from water
root_development_notes: One of the fastest and easiest plants to root; change water every 3-4 days if rooting in water to prevent bacterial buildup; shallow wide pots preferred over deep pots

rooting_indicator_1: Pup standing firm and upright on its own after 3-4 weeks
rooting_indicator_2: New leaf emerging from pup center
rooting_indicator_3: Resistance felt when gently tugging the pup
successful_rooting_signs: Plant standing firm and upright; new leaves emerging from center; no shriveling
failed_rooting_signs: Pup or leaf cutting shriveling and drying out; base turning brown and mushy; plant toppling over
root_development_notes: Allow cut end of pup or leaf to callous over for 1-3 days before planting to prevent rot; do not water for first 1-2 weeks after potting to encourage root growth; well-draining gritty mix is essential
